revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:含銅-鉄泉]]


'''Copper-Iron Spring''' (含銅-鉄泉, Gandō-Tetsusen) is a type of {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}}<nowiki/>water quality based on the {{ill|Hot Spring Types|ja|温泉の泉質|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
labeling system. It is categorized as a {{ill|Medical Spring|ja|温泉#療養泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medical Spring|1=Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q655311}}
due to its unique components.
== Overview ==
Copper-iron springs are characterized by their {{ill|oxidation|en|Redox|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/oxidation|qq=}}<nowiki/>after emergence, which causes the water to display {{ill|yellow|en|Yellow|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yellow|qq=}}
or {{ill|red|en|Red|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/red|qq=}}
hues, commonly referred to as {{ill|Red Spring|ja|赤湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Spring|qq=}}
. There are two main subtypes of copper-iron springs:
* {{ill|Bicarbonate Spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}}
* {{ill|Sulfate Spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|qq=}}
== Definition ==
A copper-iron spring is defined as containing at least 1 mg of {{ill|Copper ion|en|Copper|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Copper ion|qq=}}(Cu<sup>2+</sup>) per kg of water and belonging to the {{ill|Chalybeate|en|Chalybeate|qid=Q3046709|lt=Chalybeate springs|1=Chalybeate springs}}
category.
== Comparison of Old and New Labels ==
The classification of copper-iron springs varies widely based on the content of other components. Below is an example of the old and new water quality labels:
{| class="wikitable"
|+ Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 |qq=}}
</ref>
! Old Label !! New Label !! Abbreviated Label
|-
| Copper-Containing Acidic Vitriol Spring || Acidic - Copper & Iron (II) Sulfate Spring || Acidic･Cu･Fe(II)-SO<sub>4</sub> Spring
|}
== Health Benefits ==
''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
The following benefits are attributed to the water quality of copper-iron springs:
* '''Bathing:''' In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}}, they are considered beneficial for {{ill|Menstrual disorders|en|Menstrual disorder|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menstrual disorders|qq=}}and {{ill|Hypertension|en|Hypertension|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hypertension|qq=}}

* '''Drinking:''' Drinking copper-iron spring water is said to help with {{ill|Anemia|en|Anemia|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anemia|qq=}}

== Notes ==
<references />
== External Links ==
* {{PDF|[https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/shishin_bunseki/01.pdf Definition and Classification of Mineral Springs]|qq=}}, Ministry of the Environment, Japan

== See Also ==
* {{ill|Akayu|ja|赤湯|1=Red Spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akayu|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Spring|qq=}}

{{Onsen|qq=}}
